name: Exyn Technologies, Inc.
business: Exyn Technologies sells fully adaptive autonomous robotics and AI software for navigation and 3D mapping in GPS-denied environments, along with hardware payloads like Nexys. Revenue comes from hardware-enabled software sales, licensing, service contracts, and support. Customers include mining, construction, infrastructure, and defense sectors.

exposures.sentence: We are a pioneer in fully adaptive and cognitive mission-level autonomous robotics and artificial intelligence, delivering advanced solutions that enable safe, efficient, and scalable autonomy in complex, GPS-denied environments.
ExynAI, when coupled with our SLAM-based light detection and ranging (LiDAR) scanning technology, delivers survey-grade 3D models without a pilot.

Our wholly owned subsidiary, Exyn Defense, is a dedicated defense-focused organization that delivers ExynAI-powered autonomy and mapping software for government, national security, and allied mission applications.
We have already demonstrated measurable ROI in underground mining by reducing survey times, improving worker safety, and enabling digitization of mine planning.
Construction: Enabling digital twin creation, progress tracking, and quality assurance in megaprojects.
Space robotics: Building capabilities for GPS-denied planetary exploration, autonomous inspection of spacecraft, and participation in lunar exploration programs.
Maritime autonomy: Expanding ExynAI into subsea autonomous underwater vehicles (AUVs) and unmanned surface vessels for ocean surveying, port security, and offshore infrastructure inspection.

tamClaims.claim: Mine mapping system market forecast to reach $2.5 billion by 2033.

tamClaims.claim: Unmanned maritime systems market projected to grow from $4.9 billion to $16.0 billion by 2033.
tamClaims.sentence: According to Pro Market Reports, the unmanned maritime systems market is projected to grow from $4.9 billion to $16.0 billion by 2033, with a CAGR of approximately 14.3% between 2026 and 2033.

tamClaims.claim: Global space robotics market projected to reach $8.5 billion by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: According to Grand View Research, the global space robotics market was valued at $4.4 billion in 2022 and is projected to reach $8.5 billion by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 8.8%.

tamClaims.claim: Global commercial drone market projected to reach $54.6 billion by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: According to Grand View Research, the global commercial drone market was estimated at $30.0 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach $54.6 billion by 2030, growing at a CAGR of approximately 10.6%.

tamClaims.claim: Drone inspection and monitoring market expected to reach $31.3 billion by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: Drone inspection and monitoring... estimated at $11.8 billion in 2025 and forecast to reach $31.3 billion by 2030 as BVLOS regulations progress and sensor costs fall, according to Mordor Intelligence.

tamClaims.claim: Construction robots market expected to reach $3.7 billion by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: According to Grand View Research, the construction robots market was about $1.4 billion in 2024 and is expected to reach $3.7 billion by 2030.

tamClaims.claim: 3D mapping and modeling market projected to reach $16.8 billion by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: According to Grand View Research, the 3D mapping and 3D modeling market was $7.1 billion in 2024 and is projected to reach $16.8 billion by 2030.

tamClaims.claim: LiDAR market expected to grow to $4.71 billion by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: In parallel, the LiDAR market is expected to grow to $4.71 billion by 2030, with UAV LiDAR accelerating large-scale mapping, according to Grand View Research.

tamClaims.claim: Digital twin market expected to reach $155.8 billion by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: As owners/operators connect physical assets to live digital models, the digital twin market is expanding rapidly - from $25.0 billion in 2024 to an expected $155.8 billion by 2030 according to Grand View Research.

tamClaims.claim: Global drone market projected to grow from $73.0 billion in 2024 to $163.6 billion by 2030.
tamClaims.sentence: According to Grand View Research, the global drone market is projected to grow from $73.0 billion in 2024 to $163.6 billion by 2030, with enterprise applications and autonomy cited as key drivers.
